lessons.title: Energy Basics and Why Conservation Matters
lessons.description: Understand Zambia's energy context and why daily conservation habits matter.
lectureNotes: Energy conservation means using less electricity without reducing quality of life.
In hydro-dependent systems, drought and demand spikes can increase load shedding risk.
Small household actions scale across communities and reduce total grid pressure.

quizzes.question: What is the primary goal of energy conservation?
quizzes.explanation: Conservation focuses on reducing waste while still meeting real needs.

quizzes.question: Why are school programs important for conservation?
quizzes.explanation: Students can transfer practical habits to families and communities.

lessons.title: Home and School Energy-Saving Habits
lessons.description: Learn practical steps for lighting, appliances, and device charging.
lectureNotes: Switch off lights and fans when leaving rooms, even for short periods.
Use natural daylight where possible and group tasks to reduce repeated appliance use.
Unplug chargers and idle electronics to avoid standby power consumption.

quizzes.question: Which action reduces standby power use?
quizzes.explanation: Idle electronics can still draw power when plugged in.

quizzes.question: What is a good classroom conservation habit?
options: Keep all lights on all day
Use daylight before switching on lights
quizzes.explanation: Using available daylight reduces unnecessary electricity use.

lessons.title: Mini Energy Audit Challenge
lessons.description: Draft lesson for a simple audit activity students can run at home or school.
lectureNotes: List the top 5 devices used daily and estimate usage duration.
Identify two unnecessary uses and propose alternatives.
Track improvements for one week and compare results.

quizzes.question: What is the first step in a mini energy audit?
quizzes.explanation: Audits start by identifying where most energy is used.
